method: Within-word-shuffle null for per-position sign concentration. Over >=3-sign words (n=752), for position k in {0,1,2}: H_k = Shannon entropy (bits) of the anonymous sign distribution at position k; A_k = E_null[H] - H_k where E_null[H] is the expected per-position entropy under within-word uniform permutation of sign order (positions exchangeable => E[A_k]=0, controls sign-frequency + word-length + sample size); perm p_k = frac(null A_k >= observed A_k), one-sided (concentration), 2000 shuffles. Anonymous sign IDs only; positional entropy only; L2/L3 typology.
result: Position 1 (the second word-initial slot) is significantly concentrated beyond the within-word-shuffle null globally (A1=0.2331 bits, p=0.0000), and replicates in 2 of 6 testable sites (Zakros p=0.0015, Palaikastro p=0.0040). A concentration GRADIENT is present: A0=0.3751 > A1=0.2331 > A2=0.1278, all significant (p0=p1=0.0000, p2=0.0005). PC passed (depth-2 detectable, single-slot correctly NOT flagged at pos1, false-positive rate 0.10). Mechanical verdict: CONCENTRATION_DEPTH_2. CAVEAT: a significant pos1 concentration is structurally ambiguous between (a) a genuine second prefix morpheme (true prefix-stacking) and (b) the first prefix's selectional restriction on the following sign (E050 found A- has a weak generic continuation restriction). At L2/L3 we report only the structural fact; we do NOT adjudicate stacking-vs-selection.
key_findings: Position 1 (the SECOND word-initial slot) is significantly concentrated beyond the within-word-shuffle null GLOBALLY: A1=0.2331 bits, perm p=0.0000 (2000 shuffles). This is the structural signature of a depth-2 initial concentration, not a single prefix slot.

A monotone concentration GRADIENT exists across the first three positions: A0=0.3751 > A1=0.2331 > A2=0.1278 bits, all significant (p0=p1=0.0000, p2=0.0005). Concentration is strongest at the word edge and decays inward, consistent with prefix-zone structure rather than a single isolated slot.

The pos1 concentration REPLICATES in 2 of 6 testable sites (Zakros A1=0.331 p=0.0015; Palaikastro A1=0.481 p=0.0040), meeting the frozen >=2-site replication bar. The largest site (Haghia Triada, n=353) shows a weak same-direction effect (A1=0.073) that does not reach significance, so the depth-2 signal is site-heterogeneous rather than universal.

Positive control PASSED on all three gates: depth-2 plants were detected at BOTH pos0 and pos1 (p=0); single-slot plants were flagged at pos0 (p=0) but correctly NOT at pos1 (p=1.0); uniform plants had a 0.10 false-positive rate (<=0.10). The test discriminates depth-2 from single-slot.

INTERPRETIVE CAVEAT (frozen): a significant pos1 concentration is structurally AMBIGUOUS between (a) a genuine second prefix morpheme (true prefix-stacking) and (b) the first prefix's selectional restriction on the following sign (E050 found A- has a weak generic continuation restriction). L2/L3 reports only the structural fact; it does NOT adjudicate stacking-vs-selection.
successor_hypotheses: E068: Adjudicate stacking-vs-selection at L3. If pos1 concentration is true prefix-stacking, the pos0->pos1 bigram should show a CONCENTRATED, low-entropy transition matrix (few legal prefix-prefix pairs); if it is selectional restriction, the pos0->pos1 transition should be CONDITIONAL on pos0 identity (per-prefix continuation entropy low) but the marginal pos1 inventory still broad. Compare conditional vs marginal structure.

E069: Test whether the depth-2 signature is driven by a SMALL set of high-frequency prefix pairs (e.g. A- followed by a restricted set) vs a broad productive pattern, by jackknifing over the top-K pos0 signs and re-testing pos1 concentration.

E070: Extend the positional profile to positions 3,4,5 to map the full concentration decay curve and locate where concentration crosses zero (the prefix-zone boundary); compare the decay length to a typological null.

E071: Cross-check the site heterogeneity (Haghia Triada weak, Zakros/Palaikastro strong) against register/support (tablet vs seal vs other) to test whether depth-2 is a register-level phenomenon rather than a uniform grammatical property.

E072: Pair the positional-concentration profile with the suffix-side profile (word-FINAL positions) to test for an asymmetry prefix-zone vs suffix-zone depth, characterizing LA as prefixing-vs-suffixing at the depth-2 level.

E073: Validate the depth-2 finding against a stronger null that preserves not only within-word exchangeability but also the pos0 marginal (conditional permutation fixing pos0), to confirm pos1 concentration is not an artifact of the pos0 concentration leaking into pos1 via word-length structure.
non_circular: Anonymous sign IDs only (no readings, no values, no meaning). Metric uses positional Shannon entropy exclusively — no n-gram models, no sign identities in the test statistic. The within-word-shuffle null permutes sign order within each word, making all positions exchangeable and holding sign-frequency, word-length, and sample size fixed simultaneously, so E[A_k]=0 under the null by construction (validated by self-check: mean A over exchangeable synthetic = [-0.0027,-0.001,-0.0017]). L2/L3-typology only; no phonology, no semantics. Positive control is synthetic and stated as such.
deviations: No deviations from the frozen protocol. All steps (0-6) executed as specified: prereg+plan_hash frozen before running; PC run first and gated the verdict; global + cross-site computed with 2000 shuffles (>=1000 required); mechanical verdict applied exactly as frozen.
